name: Governance Check After Task
description: After spec task completion, verifies architecture compliance, governance artifacts, commit conventions, and DoD criteria.

then.prompt:
Check .compliance-state.json → currentPhase. Apply phase-appropriate rules only.

A spec task was just completed. Run these compliance checks:

1. ARCHITECTURE COMPLIANCE: Verify code follows architecture-principles.md — no violations of stated principles.
2. LAYER ISOLATION: If domain layer code was touched, verify no infrastructure dependencies.
3. MODULE BOUNDARIES: Verify no cross-module direct references were introduced.
4. API CONTRACT ALIGNMENT: If endpoints were created/modified, verify they match the API contract.
5. DEFINITION OF DONE: Check against DEFINITION_OF_DONE.md criteria applicable to this task.
6. COMMIT CONVENTION: Verify commits follow the stated convention from git-workflow.md.

Report findings as:
- ✅ Passing rules (silent if all pass)
- ⚠️ Warnings (non-critical gaps)
- ❌ Violations (critical rules broken — fix before PR)

If all rules pass, confirm compliance silently.

## Compliance Logging
After completing all checks above, append a JSON event to `compliance-log/events/{today-date}.jsonl` (create the file if it doesn't exist). Format:
{"timestamp": "{ISO-8601-UTC}", "type": "check", "id": "chk-{date}-{time}-{seq}", "hook": "post-task-governance", "trigger": "postTaskExecution", "ruleId": "{primary-rule-checked}", "ruleSeverity": "{severity}", "result": "{pass|fail|warn}", "message": "{one-line-finding}"}
Log ONE event per rule checked.
